message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: My feature (Add Token Position Tracking) only needs to modify tiktoken/core.py (encode function). What files does your feature need?

type: PROPOSE
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: My feature (Add Token Transformation) only touches tiktoken/core.py, specifically the encode/_encode_single_piece/_encode_bytes areas dealing with adding a transformers param to encode(). Let me know which files/functions you need so we can avoid overlap.

type: REVISE
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
your_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Both features genuinely need core.py's encode() - true file split isn't possible here. To minimize collision risk: I will ONLY touch the encode() method body/signature, adding an optional return_positions=False kwarg that returns (tokens, positions) tuple; I won't touch _encode_single_piece, _encode_bytes, or other methods. Please keep your transformers param changes as localized as possible within encode() too. We each work in our own sandbox so no live file conflicts this session - just documenting intent for merge awareness.

type: REVISE
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
your_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: We both only need tiktoken/core.py's encode() method, so a disjoint file split isn't possible here. Proposal: sequential turn-taking on the same file instead. My change is small and localized: add a  param to encode()'s signature and apply the transform(s) to  at the very top of the method body (before the allowed_special/disallowed_special logic), leaving the rest of encode() (including its return statements and the core_bpe calls) untouched. I'll go first since it's a minimal, additive change, then send DONE with the exact diff so you can layer your position-tracking changes on top of the updated file without clobbering mine. Sound good? Reply ACCEPT and I'll start immediately.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
your_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Agreed - sequential turn-taking. You go first with your transformers param at the top of encode() body, send DONE with your diff, then I'll layer return_positions on top without touching your added lines.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
your_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Agreed. I will add an optional transformers param to encode()'s signature and apply the transform chain to text at the very top of the method body only, before the allowed_special/disallowed_special logic - I won't touch return_positions, the core_bpe calls, or any other methods. Proceeding now.

type: DONE
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
your_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Finished. Added an optional 'transformers' kwarg to encode()'s signature (typed as Callable[[str], str] or Sequence of such callables) and applied it as the very first statement in the method body, before the allowed_special/disallowed_special block. Nothing else in encode() or the rest of the file was touched, so your return_positions changes should layer on cleanly. Diff is only ~25 lines, all within the signature/docstring/top-of-body of encode().

type: DONE
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
your_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Finished. Added return_positions=False kwarg to encode()'s signature; when True, computes character-start positions for each token (via decode_single_token_bytes + UTF-8 continuation-byte accounting, same technique as decode_with_offsets) and returns (tokens, positions) tuple. Only touched encode()'s signature, docstring, and the tail of its body after the existing core_bpe.encode try/except - did not touch _encode_single_piece, _encode_bytes, or any transformers-related code. Tests pass.
